STYLING: Flutter widgets (Material Design 3 + iOS Cupertino where appropriate)
PROJECT_PATHS:
- Source: `lib/`
- Entry point: `lib/main.dart`
- Core (errors, theme, utilities): `lib/core/`
- Features: `lib/features/[feature]/{data,domain,presentation}/`
- Domain layer (pure Dart, no Flutter imports): `lib/features/[feature]/domain/`
- Tests: `test/` (mirrors `lib/`)
- iOS native: `ios/`
- Android native: `android/`
- Dependencies: `pubspec.yaml`
- Lint config: `analysis_options.yaml`

DEV_COMMANDS:
- `flutter pub get` вЂ” Install dependencies from `pubspec.yaml`
- `flutter pub upgrade` вЂ” Upgrade dependencies to latest allowed versions
- `flutter run` вЂ” Run on the currently connected device/emulator
- `flutter run -d ios` вЂ” Run on iOS simulator
- `flutter run -d android` вЂ” Run on Android emulator
- `flutter build apk` вЂ” Build debug Android APK
- `flutter build appbundle` вЂ” Build Android App Bundle (Play Store)
- `flutter build ios --no-codesign` вЂ” Build iOS without signing (for CI)
- `flutter test` вЂ” Run unit and widget tests
- `flutter test --coverage` вЂ” Run tests with coverage report
- `dart analyze` вЂ” Static analysis: linting + type checking
- `dart format lib test` вЂ” Format Dart code
- `flutter clean` вЂ” Clear build artifacts and pub caches
AGENT_LIST:
- `code-reviewer` вЂ” Code review, constitution compliance, Dart type safety
- `qa-engineer` вЂ” Test writing (flutter_test + mocktail), coverage analysis, failure diagnosis
- `runtime-debugger` вЂ” Runtime error diagnosis and minimal-fix loop
- `tech-writer` вЂ” Inline dartdoc and `docs/` folder maintenance
- `security-reviewer` вЂ” OWASP MASVS, secret leaks, mobile auth flows
- `mobile-engineer` вЂ” Flutter screens, navigation, platform channels
- `architect` вЂ” Clean Architecture decisions, use cases, repositories
- `design-auditor` вЂ” Material 3 / Cupertino compliance, accessibility
- `performance-analyst` вЂ” Startup time, frame rate, app size, memory profiling
COMMIT_ATTRIBUTION:
Include AI attribution in every commit by appending this trailer:
`Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`
TYPE_SAFETY_RULES:
- No `dynamic` types without documented justification вЂ” prefer specific types or generics
- Honor null safety: use `?` for nullable, `late` only when initialization is guaranteed before first read
- Avoid unsafe casts (`as` operator) вЂ” use `is` checks or pattern matching
- No `!` (null assertion) unless the value is provably non-null at the call site
- Prefer `final`/`const` over mutable variables; immutability is the default
